isolation: Per-instance isolation on Cloudflare Containers platform (Firecracker microVMs; gVisor and QEMU also supported)
name: Cloudflare Sandboxes
notes: All disk state is ephemeral; sleeping the sandbox wipes the filesystem, and 'Snapshots are coming soon' rather than generally available.
price_headline: $0.000020 per vCPU-second (~$0.072 per vCPU-hour) plus $0.0000025 per GiB-second of memory
pricing_model: Per-second (10ms granularity), billed only while the instance is awake for active vCPU, memory, and disk
summary: Cloudflare Sandbox SDK is a managed environment for running untrusted code on behalf of AI agents, built on top of Cloudflare Containers. Each sandbox is a Linux container with a shell, filesystem, and background processes that starts on demand and resumes from where it left off. Developers drive sandboxes from a Cloudflare Worker via the @cloudflare/sandbox TypeScript SDK to execute commands, manage files, run background services, and expose preview URLs.
